scenario: Delta Bank built a BKM for risk-score computation inside a Camunda 8.8 loan decision DRD that runs 4500 evaluations per day. A second team wants to call the same BKM from an unrelated decision that lives in a different DRD inside the same project to avoid duplicating the FEEL logic.
question: Which constraint applies when the second team tries to reuse the BKM in Camunda 8.8 from another decision?

options.text: A BKM can be reused only within the same decision in Camunda 8.8 and is not shared between separate decisions
A BKM can be reused freely across any DRDs once it has been saved and given a unique name inside the project
A BKM can be reused across decisions only if its parameters and result type match the other decision exactly
A BKM can be reused across decisions only after it has been wrapped in a literal expression in each consumer

a.text: Correct. The docs explicitly state that, currently, you can only reuse BKMs within the same decision, so the second team in a different decision cannot reuse the existing BKM directly.

b.text: Incorrect. The docs cap BKM reuse to the same decision; they do not describe a project-wide reuse model triggered by unique naming or saving.

c.text: Incorrect. The docs do not condition BKM reuse on parameter and result-type matching across decisions; the constraint is the decision boundary itself.

d.text: Incorrect. The docs describe a BKM as already executable through a literal expression; they do not require an extra wrapper literal expression on the consumer side.

explanation: In Camunda 8.8, a BKM is currently reusable only within the same decision. A BKM defined in one decision is not available to a different decision, even within the same DRD or project, so cross-decision reuse is not possible.
